preamble: The palatal nasal /ɲ/ can undergo a phonetic transformation to a palatalized alveolar nasal /nʲ/ through a process of depalatalization or a merger with an existing /nj/ sequence. This shift involves the primary articulation moving from the hard palate to the alveolar ridge, with the palatalization becoming a secondary articulation.

examples.note: The palatal nasal in words like 'huraño' (unsociable) merges with the /nj/ sequence, often realized as a palatalized alveolar nasal [nʲ].

sources: Colantoni, L., & Gurlekian, J. (2021). An acoustical analysis of the merger of /ɲ/ and /nj/ in Buenos Aires Spanish. Journal of the International Phonetic Association, 51(2), 241-262.
